string.text: The FG42, also known as the Fallschirmjagergewehr 42, was a selective-fire battle rifle used by the German paratroopers during World War II. Designed in the early 1940s, this innovative rifle combined the features of a light machine gun and a rifle, providing soldiers with a versatile weapon for various combat situations. It featured a distinctive top-mounted magazine and a folding bipod, allowing for both accurate sustained fire and traditional rifle-style shooting. The FG42 was prized for its compact design and firepower, making it ideal for airborne troops who needed a lightweight and efficient weapon. Its unique design elements influenced later firearm designs, and it remains a notable piece of military history.\n %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:\n %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] FG-42 Scope
Scope developed for the FG-42 sniper rifle, despite it's age, the construction is impeccable, making it a great scope for long range accurate shooting.
Standard magazine for the FG-42 sniper rifle, it holds 20 rounds of 7.62x57 ammunition.
